title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new premium subscription to a music streaming service.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally upgraded to the 'Hi-Fi' tier on my music app. The difference in sound clarity is actually quite startling. || Woman: [1]I’m still not convinced the average ear can tell the difference[/1], especially when you're just using standard earbuds on a noisy bus. || Man: You need proper headphones to appreciate it. But I agree, it’s a luxury. || Woman: [2]What I do like, though, is the new 'offline mode' for high-res files[/2]. It’s great for when I’m travelling through areas with patchy signal. || Man: Definitely. It saves a lot of frustration. Though I think they could make the interface a bit less cluttered. || Woman: True, it takes way too many clicks just to find my downloaded playlists lately.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally upgraded to the 'Hi-Fi' tier on my music app. The difference in sound clarity is actually quite startling.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’m still not convinced the average ear can tell the difference, especially when you're just using standard earbuds on a noisy bus.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You need proper headphones to appreciate it. But I agree, it’s a luxury.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I do like, though, is the new 'offline mode' for high-res files. It’s great for when I’m travelling through areas with patchy signal.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Definitely. It saves a lot of frustration. Though I think they could make the interface a bit less cluttered.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: True, it takes way too many clicks just to find my downloaded playlists lately.

questions.question: The woman expresses skepticism regarding

options.A: the value of the audio improvements.
options.B: the reliability of the streaming app.
options.C: the cost of the premium subscription.

questions.explanation: She isn't convinced that the 'average ear' can notice the change in quality [1].

questions.question: What feature of the service does the woman find particularly useful?

options.B: the ability to access content without internet
options.C: the streamlined design of the user interface

questions.explanation: She praises the 'offline mode' for when signal is 'patchy' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a professional development seminar they attended.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That session on 'Time Blocking' was quite a revelation. I’ve already cleared my Tuesday morning for deep work. || Woman: It was okay. But [3]I suspect the speaker was a bit out of touch with how reactive our roles actually are[/3]. We can't just ignore the phones for four hours. || Man: I suppose it depends on the department. It seemed to work for the dev team. || Woman: [4]I was disappointed that there wasn't more time for the Q&A at the end[/4]. I had a specific question about managing shared calendars that never got answered. || Man: I agree. He talked for twenty minutes longer than he was supposed to. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a bit ironic for a time-management expert to run over his own schedule.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That session on 'Time Blocking' was quite a revelation. I’ve already cleared my Tuesday morning for deep work.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was okay. But I suspect the speaker was a bit out of touch with how reactive our roles actually are. We can't just ignore the phones for four hours.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I suppose it depends on the department. It seemed to work for the dev team.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was disappointed that there wasn't more time for the Q&A at the end. I had a specific question about managing shared calendars that never got answered.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. He talked for twenty minutes longer than he was supposed to.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a bit ironic for a time-management expert to run over his own schedule.

questions.question: The woman suggests that the seminar's advice was
questions.explanation: She feels the speaker didn't realize how 'reactive' (requiring quick responses) her role is [3].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the seminar

options.B: was held in an unsuitable location.

questions.explanation: They both highlight that the Q&A session was cut short because the speaker ran over [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new local bakery.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you tried the sourdough from 'The Crust'? It’s definitely the best in the neighbourhood. || Woman: It is good. But [5]I found the queuing system a bit chaotic[/5]. There was no clear line and people were just hovering around the counter. || Man: It’s a bit of a victim of its own success, I think. They weren't prepared for the crowds. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate that they’ve kept the prices quite reasonable[/6], though, especially compared to that fancy place by the station. || Man: I agree. It feels like a proper community shop rather than a tourist trap. || Woman: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to go a bit earlier next time to avoid the mid-morning rush.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you tried the sourdough from 'The Crust'? It’s definitely the best in the neighbourhood.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is good. But I found the queuing system a bit chaotic. There was no clear line and people were just hovering around the counter.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It’s a bit of a victim of its own success, I think. They weren't prepared for the crowds.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate that they’ve kept the prices quite reasonable, though, especially compared to that fancy place by the station.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It feels like a proper community shop rather than a tourist trap.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to go a bit earlier next time to avoid the mid-morning rush.

questions.question: What did the woman dislike about her visit to the bakery?

options.A: the quality of the customer service
options.B: the lack of organization in the shop
options.C: the limited range of products available

questions.explanation: She describes the 'queuing system' as 'chaotic' with no clear line [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the bakery's
questions.explanation: They both agree that the prices are 'reasonable' and not like a 'tourist trap' [6].
